extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI Michal attacked solo on the penultimate gravel sector with 2km remaining to win by 20-30 seconds over pursuing group of seven.
tactical_patterns: KWIATKOWSKI's stealth attack came transitioning OFF gravel onto tarmac, exploiting positioning chaos during surface change with 2km left.
Sky used BOASSON HAGEN and DEL MULAN to shred chase group on gravel, isolating rivals before KWIATKOWSKI's final move.
Penultimate gravel sector proved more decisive than final sector—attacks with two sectors remaining created unbridgeable gaps.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Former world champion timed solo move perfectly at gravel-to-tarmac transition, suggesting superior wet gravel handling
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: Consistent second place finish indicates strong positioning but unable to respond to late gravel attacks
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: First podium suggests Lotto-Soudal identified wet conditions as opportunity for rouleur types over pure climbers
rider_signals.STYBAR Zdenek: negative: Quick-Step rider shook head after fourth, indicating team expectation gap despite gravel expertise
